_note: This file is a design-time snapshot and may be out of date. For current step state, run: iw item-status <ID> --json. The DB is the authoritative source of truth (CR-00023).
title: Recent Activity messages need truncation + click-to-expand popup

steps.description: Truncate Recent Activity message to 100 chars + '...' on the project dashboard; add a generic activity-text modal partial; render full text as a hidden payload only when truncation occurred; small JS for click/ESC/click-outside dismissal and focus return. Messages of 100 chars or fewer render unchanged with no '...' and no click affordance.

Review S01: truncation cutoff is exactly 100 codepoints, suffix is literal '...', short-message path is unchanged, escape safety, accessibility (focus trap + return), no regressions on entity-link routing for batch/doc_job/work_item rows, no collision with oss-finding-modal IDs.

Add tests/integration/test_i00067_recent_activity_truncation.py: (a) 200-char message renders 100 chars + '...' AND full text is in the modal payload; (b) 100-char message renders verbatim with NO '...' and NO trigger affordance; (c) 99-char and 101-char boundary cases; (d) entity-link URLs for batch/doc_job/work_item rows are unchanged. Assertions check specific values, not shape.
Review S03: tests are falsifiable on main (would fail without the fix), specific-value assertions, no shape-only checks, no flakiness, fixtures clean up.

Global review across S01 + S03: template + modal partial + tests integrate end-to-end; the regression test really exercises the broken path; no other consumers of the activity rendering regressed; full unit + integration suite passes.
Self-assessment of the just-completed item via the iw-item-analyze skill
QV: Browser verification — confirm truncation and the click-to-expand popup work end-to-end in the isolated worktree stack
